message:
feat(snapshot_engine): Cx-first mark_shutdown_with_cx + run_periodic_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Close snapshot_engine.rs's 2 cx gaps on the SnapshotEngine:

1. `mark_shutdown_with_cx(cx)` — two checkpoint seams:
   - Pre-flight before acquiring the session-id read lock
   - After spawn_blocking DB update returns (so a late-cancelled
     caller gets the cancel surface without dropping DB work
     in-flight)
   Returns `SnapshotError::Cancelled` on pre-cancelled cx without
   touching the DB.

2. `run_periodic_with_cx(cx, shutdown, pane_provider)` — pre-flight
   checkpoint gates the scheduler-loop entry before the shutdown
   receiver setup or startup capture fires. Delegates to
   `run_periodic` for the scheduler body (deep cx threading through
   both Periodic/Intelligent scheduling modes would duplicate ~150
   lines; a future tick may lift that). The pre-flight seam is still
   valuable because `run_periodic` is the supervisor-spawned entry
   point for the snapshot engine — a cancelled supervisor cx bails
   before the scheduler arms itself.

Legacy `mark_shutdown` + `run_periodic` preserved unchanged.

Two regression tests pin `mark_shutdown_with_cx`:
- `mark_shutdown_with_cx_sets_flag` — fresh cx sets shutdown_clean
  flag identically to legacy path
- `mark_shutdown_with_precancelled_cx_returns_cancelled` —
  pre-cancelled cx returns `SnapshotError::Cancelled` without DB
  write

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features asupersync-runtime): 91/91
snapshot_engine tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
